message:
bd-xox.7: Tracing span hierarchy enforcement tests

Add 28 unit tests verifying canonical tracing span hierarchy for the
standard render cycle. Tests cover:
- Root span identification (init, update, frame, subscriptions, conformal, golden)
- Parent-child nesting (view→frame, present→frame, inline→present, etc.)
- Required fields on all span types per telemetry-events.md spec
- No orphan spans (every non-root span has a valid parent)
- Deterministic span ordering (view before present, scroll→diff→emit)
- Span level correctness (INFO vs DEBUG per spec)
- Cross-domain independence (conformal/golden are independent roots)
- Multiple update variants are siblings, not nested

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"bd-3bxhj.10","title":"epic(opentui-import): Alien-graveyard uplift for synthesis, formal assurance, and shadow-run adoption","description":"Background:
This epic operationalizes alien-graveyard + alien-artifact-coding methods so OpenTUI→FrankenTUI migration is not heuristic glue code, but a formally constrained, executable, replayable decision system with strong safety/fallback behavior.

North star:
Convert advanced math and systems ideas into production-grade, deterministic migration policy that improves outcomes while remaining auditable and operator-safe.

Capability pillars:
1) Contracts + provenance:
- Contracts-as-code engine, artifact-graph IDs, claim/evidence/policy/trace linkage.
2) Formal decision core:
- Closed-form Bayesian posterior + expected-loss policy + formal guarantee wrappers.
3) Graveyard executable workflow:
- Symptom-router/Fast-Start harvesting, composability/risk matrices, entry-header/graduation schema, graveyardctl verification.
4) Robust artifact-coding extensions:
- Dependence-aware conjugate fusion, sequential FDR wealth control, minimal-flip counterfactual audits, graceful degradation compiler, assumption applicability checker, L0-L3 galaxy-brain contracts.
5) Testing and proof gates:
- Deep unit/property suites + end-to-end gauntlets with deterministic replay artifacts and red-path safety diagnostics.
6) Release enforcement:
- RC promotion blocked unless all mandatory alien + optimization + certification gauntlets pass and artifact clauses are machine-valid.

Execution map (high level):
- Foundation + governance track: .10.1-.10.20
- Formal inference/guarantee track: .10.21-.10.28
- Graveyard executable track: .10.29-.10.37
- Advanced artifact-coding track: .10.38-.10.45

Success criteria:
1) Every advanced primitive/controller has budgeted mode, deterministic fallback, and replayable evidence traces.
2) Recommendation contracts are machine-linted for EV/tier, metadata schema, risk countermeasures, guarantees, comparator, rollback, and demo linkage completeness.
3) Mandatory gauntlets pass for route→rank→contract→verify→demo→release pipelines before RC promotion.
4) CI fails on missing artifacts, unsafe composition, violated assumptions, invalid guarantees, or policy-breaking optimizations.
5) Operators can reconstruct any migration decision (and nearest counterfactual flip) from structured logs alone.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:49:38.728311183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:46:25.850012731Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","formal-methods","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:49:38.728311183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"bd-3bxhj.10.38","title":"task(opentui-import alien): Implement hierarchical conjugate Bayesian evidence fusion with dependence-aware corrections","description":"Background:
OpenTUI→FrankenTUI migration evidence streams are heterogeneous, sparse in long tails, and often correlated (e.g., widget-coverage signal correlates with runtime-event signal). Naive score fusion overstates certainty and creates brittle decisions.

Goal:
Build an alien-artifact-grade evidence-fusion core that is closed-form, deterministic, and auditable under correlated weak signals.

Work packages:
1) Conjugate online kernels:
- Beta-Binomial for Bernoulli outcomes (pass/fail checks, parity flags).
- Gamma-Poisson for rate processes (event/error intensity).
- Dirichlet-Multinomial for categorical strategy outcomes.
2) Hierarchical shrinkage:
- Partial pooling across project family, component class, and widget archetype.
- Empirical-Bayes prior initialization from corpus strata to reduce variance in sparse regimes.
3) Dependence-aware fusion:
- Correlation/copula-inspired correction term to deflate double-counted evidence.
- Posterior covariance proxy exported with each fused decision.
4) Robustification:
- Huberized likelihood fallback for outlier-heavy telemetry.
- Prior-stress hooks (epsilon-contamination / prior-perturbation probes) to quantify model-risk sensitivity.
5) Diagnostics + audit artifacts:
- Posterior predictive checks and simulation-based calibration (SBC) harness entry-points.
- Deterministic evidence hash + ledger rows for replay and explainability cards.

Deliverables:
- Runtime fusion module integrated with existing evidence ledger contracts.
- JSONL artifact schema additions: posterior_state_id, shrinkage_profile_id, dependence_factor, robust_mode, sensitivity_band.
- Operator-facing diagnostics for mismatch and overconfidence detection.

Acceptance criteria:
1) All posterior updates are replay-stable and numerically stable in log-domain at extreme values.
2) Correlated channels never increase confidence without explicit dependence adjustment trace.
3) Sensitivity bands are emitted for each high-impact decision; high sensitivity auto-raises conservative action policy.
4) Predictive-check failures trigger explicit degraded-confidence flags consumed by downstream policy gates.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:02.741504654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:02.512214829Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","bayesian","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.38","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:02.741504654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.38","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:01.635158562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.38","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.23","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:02.073744935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.38","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:02.512136953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3bxhj.10.39","title":"task(opentui-import alien): Build sequential multiple-testing control with e-BH + alpha-investing wealth management","description":"Background:
The migration pipeline evaluates many hypotheses over time (quality regressions, parity claims, release-worthiness checks). Classical fixed-horizon p-value logic breaks under optional stopping and asynchronous gate order.

Goal:
Implement anytime-valid, multi-hypothesis control that preserves statistical validity while remaining operationally practical.

Work packages:
1) Sequential evidence core:
- e-value constructors for each gate family (quality, performance, accessibility, determinism).
- Safe composition rules for sequential and batched updates.
2) Multiple-testing control:
- Primary controller: e-BH over concurrent gate families.
- Secondary policy profiles: alpha-investing wealth process for online arrival of tests.
- Optional advanced profile hooks for LORD/SAFFRON-style online FDR where appropriate.
3) Wealth governance:
- Per-family wealth ledgers with transfer/clamp policies.
- Starvation prevention and fairness budgeting between high- and low-volume gate families.
4) Policy integration:
- Hard fallback rules when wealth is exhausted or evidence is adversarially noisy.
- Deterministic replay from event stream with no order ambiguity.
5) Observability:
- Structured artifacts for e_value, rejection_threshold, wealth_before, wealth_after, family_id, and decision clause.

Deliverables:
- Runtime sequential-FDR controller package with policy config schema.
- Audit trail and explainability surfaces for wealth trajectory and rejection rationales.
- Gate adapter for release policy consumption.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Type-I/FDR control remains valid under optional stopping and arbitrary asynchronous execution order.
2) Wealth process is deterministic and replay-identical for fixed seed/input logs.
3) Budget depletion always triggers explicitly logged conservative mode (never silent permissive behavior).
4) Controller emits machine-checkable clauses linking each reject/retain decision to e-value and wealth state.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:03.177459819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:03.836500424Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","e-process","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.39","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:03.177459819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.39","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:03.393384970Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.39","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.23","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:02.950000322Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.39","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:03.836459598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"bd-3bxhj.10.40","title":"task(opentui-import alien): Implement counterfactual decision-audit solver and minimal-flip explanations","description":"Background:
Users and operators need decision transparency that is not hand-wavy: for each migration verdict, we must be able to answer what minimally would have needed to change for a different action to be optimal.

Goal:
Provide formal counterfactual decision audits with minimal-flip explanations that are reproducible and policy-aware.

Work packages:
1) Counterfactual problem formalization:
- Encode decision rule as constrained optimization over evidence terms and loss matrix parameters.
- Support L1/L2/weighted perturbation norms and policy constraints (immutable evidence, bounded edits).
2) Solver layer:
- Deterministic minimal-perturbation search (MILP/SAT-compatible abstraction where needed).
- Tie-break policy for multiple equivalent perturbation sets.
3) Action-sensitivity analysis:
- Compute nearest alternative action and perturbation distance.
- Emit feature-level and group-level perturbation attributions.
4) Governance integration:
- Attach counterfactual artifact IDs to decision claim records.
- Provide blocking rule when decision is excessively fragile (small perturbation norm below configured threshold).
5) UX + audit exposure:
- JSON/ledger output + human-readable summary for explainability cards and review workflows.

Deliverables:
- Counterfactual engine with deterministic solver settings.
- Artifact schema: decision_id, baseline_action, alt_action, perturbation_set, perturbation_norm, satisfiable, fragility_band.
- Policy hook to require mitigation when fragility is high.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Every promoted decision has at least one satisfiable minimal-flip counterfactual or an explicit unsat proof artifact.
2) Re-running the solver on identical input yields identical perturbation set and norm.
3) Fragile decisions trigger explicit policy clauses and cannot bypass release gates silently.
4) Counterfactual artifacts are linked to claim/policy IDs and replay commands.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:03.605553882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:05.170678145Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","explainability","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.40","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:03.605553882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.40","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:04.725658525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.40","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:04.287830502Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.40","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.33","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:05.170629704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3bxhj.10.41","title":"task(opentui-import alien): Build robust missing-evidence graceful-degradation policy compiler","description":"Background:
Real migration runs regularly face partial telemetry, delayed traces, contradictory probes, and MNAR-style missingness. We need graceful degradation that preserves safety and debuggability instead of brittle failure.

Goal:
Compile explicit degradation policy contracts so decisions remain safe, interpretable, and recoverable under incomplete evidence.

Work packages:
1) Degradation DSL:
- Encode missingness classes (MCAR/MAR/MNAR proxies), contradiction classes, and evidence-quality tiers.
- Specify action clamps, uncertainty inflation rules, and max-risk envelopes per tier.
2) Robust inference fallback:
- Deterministic imputation families with provenance tags (carry-forward, conservative priors, bounded worst-case).
- Confidence inflation and loss penalization under degraded evidence.
3) State machine:
- Entry/exit/cooldown semantics for degraded mode.
- Hysteresis thresholds to prevent oscillation between normal/degraded states.
4) Adversarial resilience:
- Contradictory-evidence arbitration rules and anomaly-triggered clamp mode.
- Escalation hooks for manual operator review in ambiguous red zones.
5) Recovery proofs:
- Criteria for re-entering full-confidence mode tied to measurable evidence-quality improvements.

Deliverables:
- Degradation policy compiler + runtime evaluator.
- Artifact schema: degradation_reason, evidence_quality_score, inflation_factor, clamp_action, recovery_guard.
- Deterministic replay traces for degraded-path decisions.

Acceptance criteria:
1) No decision path fails catastrophically when any configured evidence subset is missing/delayed/contradictory.
2) Every degraded decision emits explicit reason codes, uncertainty deltas, and clamp clauses.
3) Recovery to normal mode requires satisfied measurable guards; no implicit auto-promotion.
4) Degraded paths remain deterministic and auditable end-to-end.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:04.047412733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:06.542960525Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","graceful-degradation","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.41","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:04.047412733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.41","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:06.083002159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.41","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:06.542910421Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.41","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.38","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:05.620397116Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3bxhj.10.42","title":"task(opentui-import alien): Implement guarantee-assumption registry and automatic applicability checker","description":"Background:
Formal guarantees are only trustworthy when their assumptions are explicit and continuously checked (exchangeability, stationarity windows, bounded drift, contamination limits, independence approximations).

Goal:
Create a first-class assumption/guarantee contract layer that prevents invalid guarantee claims from entering release decisions.

Work packages:
1) Assumption registry:
- Typed assumption catalog with IDs, formal definitions, observables, and violation thresholds.
- Mapping from each guarantee mechanism (conformal/e-process/PAC-Bayes/sequential-FDR) to required assumption sets.
2) Applicability checker:
- Runtime and gate-time evaluators computing assumption satisfaction scores from telemetry/evidence logs.
- Contradiction/falsification detectors for unstable regimes.
3) Guarantee downgrade policy:
- Automatic downgrade/invalidation rules when assumptions are violated.
- Conservative fallback action mapping for each downgraded guarantee class.
4) Proof artifacts:
- Emit per-decision guarantee certificate including assumption hash, applicability verdict, and downgrade rationale.
- Cross-link guarantee certificate with decision claim + release gate records.
5) Operator diagnostics:
- Human-readable plus machine-checkable reports for why a guarantee is valid/invalid.

Deliverables:
- Assumption registry schema + checker engine.
- Artifact schema additions: guarantee_id, assumption_set_hash, applicability_score, violated_assumptions, fallback_clause.
- Integration contract for release policy and explainability surfaces.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Every guarantee claim in the pipeline references explicit assumption IDs and a computed applicability verdict.
2) Assumption violations block unsafe promotion paths and force conservative fallback with logged rationale.
3) Guarantee certificates are reproducible and replay-identical for identical inputs.
4) No guarantee can be surfaced as 'valid' without passing checker thresholds.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:04.495423705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:07.911743952Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","formal-guarantees","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.42","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:04.495423705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.42","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.23","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:06.998532868Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.42","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.31","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:07.458366861Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.42","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:07.911702164Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3bxhj.10.43","title":"task(opentui-import alien): Elevate galaxy-brain UX to L0-L3 with interaction, accessibility, and performance contracts","description":"Background:
Alien-grade math only helps users if it is comprehensible and operationally safe. Explainability must be progressive, deterministic, and explicitly non-interfering with decision outputs.

Goal:
Ship L0-L3 galaxy-brain UX contracts that make the inference stack transparent without sacrificing performance, accessibility, or determinism.

L0-L3 contract:
- L0: decision signal + confidence band + risk class.
- L1: plain-language intuition and dominant evidence contributors.
- L2: structured evidence terms, thresholds, and guarantee status.
- L3: full equations, substituted values, and machine-exportable artifacts.

Work packages:
1) Contract schema for card content IDs, stability hashes, and provenance links.
2) Interaction model: keyboard-first navigation, deterministic expand/collapse, copy-as Unicode/LaTeX/JSON.
3) Non-interference guard: enforce presentation-only behavior (no feedback into decision core).
4) Accessibility contract: contrast/readability checks, screen-reader-friendly text alternatives, low-motion mode.
5) Performance budget: render-latency and allocation caps for L0-L3 transitions under stress fixtures.
6) Diff stability: deterministic card ordering and content hashing for replay comparison.

Deliverables:
- Galaxy-brain card schema + renderer contract + deterministic IDs.
- UX telemetry for interaction latency and accessibility compliance verdicts.
- CI checks for non-interference, perf budgets, and deterministic rendering.

Acceptance criteria:
1) L0-L3 transitions are deterministic and produce stable content hashes for identical evidence.
2) Explainability surfaces cannot mutate or perturb decision outputs (hard non-interference).
3) Accessibility/performance contracts pass under representative and adversarial fixtures.

+{"id":"bd-3bxhj.10.44","title":"task(opentui-import alien tests): Unit/property tests for conjugate fusion, sequential FDR control, counterfactuals, and degradation policies","description":"Background:
The new artifact-coding layers are mathematically dense and easy to regress silently. We need exhaustive deterministic tests that validate numeric stability, policy safety, and replay invariants.

Goal:
Deliver comprehensive unit/property test coverage for beads .10.38-.10.43 with rich diagnostics for fast triage.

Test matrix:
1) Fusion kernel tests:
- Extreme-value log-domain stability, sparse regimes, shrinkage sanity, dependence correction monotonicity.
- Robust-mode behavior under outliers and contamination stress.
2) Sequential-FDR tests:
- Optional-stopping invariants, async order invariance, wealth conservation/limits, family fairness.
3) Counterfactual tests:
- Minimal-flip optimality checks, deterministic tie-breaks, unsat proof-path validation.
4) Degradation tests:
- Missing/contradictory evidence scenarios, clamp correctness, hysteresis and recovery guards.
5) Assumption-check tests:
- Guarantee validity/invalidity transitions, downgrade policy correctness, certificate integrity.
6) UX contract tests:
- Non-interference assertions, deterministic card IDs, accessibility/perf threshold checks.

Deliverables:
- Unit + property test suites with deterministic seed control.
- Golden artifacts/log snapshots for replay and contract validation.
- Failure output normalized for machine triage and human debugging.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Coverage includes happy path, edge cases, adversarial inputs, and failure-mode policy paths for each artifact module.
2) Property tests prove deterministic decisions/artifacts under equivalent evidence permutations.
3) Failures always emit rich structured logs: equation_id, assumption_id, guarantee_id, wealth_state, perturbation_norm, degradation_reason, replay_cmd.
4) Test suites integrate with existing CI gates and remain reproducible on rerun.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:05.374342791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:59.958630855Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","opentui-import","test","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.44","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:13.380367210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.44","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:05.374342791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.44","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:12.916583450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.44","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:59.958577445Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.44","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.38","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:10.175625384Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.44","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.39","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:10.635954324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.44","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.40","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:11.096573588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.44","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.41","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:11.556599682Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.44","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.42","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:12.010568882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.44","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.43","type":"blocks","created_ +{"id":"bd-3bxhj.10.45","title":"task(opentui-import alien tests): E2E alien-artifact deep-assurance gauntlet (streaming updates, FDR, counterfactuals, graceful degradation, UX)","description":"Background:
Unit tests alone cannot prove operational correctness across the full migration pipeline. We need a world-class E2E gauntlet that stress-tests sequential uncertainty, policy interaction, and explainability artifacts under realistic and adversarial flows.

Goal:
Create a deep-assurance E2E gauntlet that must pass before release-candidate promotion.

Scenario families:
1) Streaming evidence fusion:
- Online conjugate updates with hierarchical shrinkage and dependence correction across long-running sessions.
- Posterior predictive checks + sensitivity bands under drift and sparse windows.
2) Sequential multi-testing:
- Interleaved asynchronous gates using e-BH + alpha-investing under optional stopping and reorderings.
3) Counterfactual + fragility drills:
- Minimal-flip explanation generation for each critical decision; fragile decisions force mitigation path.
4) Degradation and recovery:
- MNAR/missing/contradictory evidence campaigns, clamp-mode safety, and recovery threshold enforcement.
5) Guarantee applicability faults:
- Assumption violations injected mid-run; guarantee downgrade/invalidation and conservative fallback verified.
6) Galaxy-brain contracts:
- L0-L3 deterministic rendering, accessibility/perf budgets, and hard non-interference checks.

Deliverables:
- End-to-end scripts plus fixture bundles for green-path and red-path campaigns.
- Deterministic JSONL artifact pack containing posterior, wealth, guarantee, counterfactual, degradation, and UX records.
- Automated replay/triage helpers with concise failure signatures and reproduction commands.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Entire gauntlet is deterministic for fixed fixtures/seeds and emits reproducible evidence bundles.
2) Red-path scenarios fail safely with explicit machine-checkable diagnostic clauses.
3) Passing this gauntlet is mandatory for release-candidate promotion alongside existing alien/optimization/certification gates.
4) E2E logs are rich enough for one-command replay and root-cause localization without manual forensics.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:05.823012246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:16.085304228Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","e2e","opentui-import","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.45","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:15.627200285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.45","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:05.823012246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.45","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:15.179295782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.45","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.34","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:14.724500725Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.45","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.37","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:14.285435506Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.45","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.44","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:13.832397389Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.45","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:16.085248003Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3cc","title":"Implement Frame struct (Buffer + metadata)","description":"# Implement Frame struct (Buffer + metadata)

## Background
Plan Section 6.1 defines Frame as the container for a render pass: the Buffer grid plus metadata needed for presentation and interaction.

## Frame Purpose
Frame is the "render target" that view() methods write to. It bundles:
1. The cell grid (Buffer)
2. Optional hit testing grid (HitGrid)
3. Cursor metadata

## Structure

```rust
/// Frame = Buffer + metadata for a render pass
/// 
/// # Design Rationale
/// Frame is passed to Model::view() and contains everything needed to render.
/// The Buffer holds cells; metadata controls cursor and enables mouse hit testing.
/// 
/// Frame does NOT own pools (GraphemePool, LinkRegistry) - those are passed separately
/// or accessed via RenderContext to allow sharing across frames.
pub struct Frame {
    /// The cell grid for this render pass
    pub buffer: Buffer,
    
    /// Optional hit grid for mouse hit testing
    /// When Some, widgets can register clickable regions
    pub hit_grid: Option<HitGrid>,
    
    /// Cursor position (if app wants to show cursor)
    /// Coordinates are relative to buffer (0-indexed)
    pub cursor_position: Option<(u16, u16)>,
    
    /// Whether cursor should be visible
    pub cursor_visible: bool,
}
```

## Frame Lifecycle

```rust
impl Frame {
    /// Create a new frame with given dimensions
    pub fn new(width: u16, height: u16) -> Self {
        Self {
            buffer: Buffer::new(width, height),
            hit_grid: None,
            cursor_position: None,
            cursor_visible: true,
        }
    }
    
    /// Create frame with hit testing enabled
    pub fn with_hit_grid(width: u16, height: u16) -> Self {
        Self {
            buffer: Buffer::new(width, height),
            hit_grid: Some(HitGrid::new(width, height)),
            cursor_position: None,
            cursor_visible: true,
        }
    }
    
    /// Dimensions
    pub fn width(&self) -> u16 { self.buffer.width() }
    pub fn height(&self) -> u16 { self.buffer.height() }
    
    /// Clear frame for next render
    pub fn clear(&mut self) {
        self.buffer.clear();
        if let Some(ref mut grid) = self.hit_grid {
            grid.clear();
        }
    }
    
    /// Set cursor position (None to hide)
    pub fn set_cursor(&mut self, position: Option<(u16, u16)>) {
        self.cursor_position = position;
    }
    
    /// Show/hide cursor
    pub fn set_cursor_visible(&mut self, visible: bool) {
        self.cursor_visible = visible;
    }
}
```

## Integration Points

### With Model::view()
```rust
trait Model {
    fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame);
}

impl Model for MyApp {
    fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
        // Draw widgets to frame.buffer
        // Register hit regions with frame.hit_grid
        // Set cursor position if needed
        self.status_bar.render(frame);
        frame.set_cursor(Some((self.cursor_x, self.cursor_y)));
    }
}
```

### With Presenter
```rust
// Runtime uses Frame for diff and presentation
let diff = BufferDiff::compute(&prev_buffer, &frame.buffer);
presenter.present(&frame.buffer, &diff, &pool, &links)?;

// Handle cursor separately
if frame.cursor_visible {
    if let Some((x, y)) = frame.cursor_position {
        presenter.position_cursor(x, y)?;
    }
    presenter.show_cursor()?;
} else {
    presenter.hide_cursor()?;
}
```

## HitGrid (Optional Feature)
Hit grid enables mouse interaction by mapping screen positions to widget IDs.

```rust
/// Hit testing grid for mouse interaction
pub struct HitGrid {
    width: u16,
    height: u16,
    cells: Vec<Option<HitId>>,
}

/// Identifier for a clickable region
#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct HitId(pub u32);

impl HitGrid {
    pub fn register(&mut self, rect: Rect, id: HitId) {
        for y in rect.y..(rect.y + rect.height) {
            for x in rect.x..(rect.x + rect.width) {
                if let Some(cell) = self.get_mut(x, y) {
                    *cell = Some(id);
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    pub fn hit_test(&self, x: u16, y: u16) -> Option<HitId> {
        self.get(x, y).and_then(|c| *c)
    }
}
```

## Tests Required

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    
    #[test]
    fn test_frame_creation() {
        let frame = Frame::new(80, 24);
        assert_eq\!(frame.width(), 80);
        assert_eq\!(frame.height(), 24);
        assert\!(frame.hit_grid.is_none());
        assert\!(frame.cursor_position.is_none());
        assert\!(frame.cursor_visible);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_frame_with_hit_grid() {
        let frame = Frame::with_hit_grid(80, 24);
        assert\!(frame.hit_grid.is_some());
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_frame_cursor() {
        let mut frame = Frame::new(80, 24);
        frame.set_cursor(Some((10, 5)));
        assert_eq\!(frame.cursor_position, Some((10, 5)));
        
        frame.set_cursor_visible(false);
        assert\!(\!frame.cursor_visible);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_hit_grid_registration() {
        let mut frame = Frame::with_hit_grid(80, 24);
        let hit_id = HitId(42);
        let rect = Rect::new(10, 5, 20, 3);
        
        if let Some(ref mut grid) = frame.hit_grid {
            grid.register(rect, hit_id);
            
            // Inside rect
            assert_eq\!(grid.hit_test(15, 6), Some(hit_id));
            
            // Outside rect
            assert_eq\!(grid.hit_test(5, 5), None);
        }
    }
}
```

- [ ] Cursor position and visibility tracked
- [ ] HitGrid registration and hit testing work
- [ ] clear() resets both buffer and hit grid
- [ ] Tests pass
